mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.8% in Sept 2009 reflects post-crisis labor market weakness
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volume (RSAFS) indicates accelerating consumer demand and inventory replenishment cycles, signaling near-term earnings upgrades and positive equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344.7B in Sept 2009 reflects early recovery phase
failure_condition: When RSAFS rises > 2% MoM, SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals monetary accommodation and real rate compression, reducing equity risk premium and supporting equity valuations over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% and CPIAUCSL at 215.79 in Sept 2009
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 62% of instances, or the joint signal shows negative correlation with forward returns (Rank IC < -0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) reflects capacity utilization recovery and manufacturing momentum, signaling improved corporate profitability and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.44 in Aug 2009 shows post-recession recovery
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises > 1% MoM, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand recovery; when both accelerate simultaneously, it indicates sustained consumer and construction activity driving equity valuations higher over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 3% MoM AND RSAFS rises > 1.5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 598K and RSAFS at 344.7B in Aug-Sept 2009
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 65% of instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² increase < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) accelerates above 150K monthly additions, signaling labor market tightening and wage pressure that supports consumer spending and equity earnings over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.9M in Sept 2009 reflects ongoing post-crisis employment weakness
regime_hint: labor market recovery phase with accelerating job creation
failure_condition: When monthly PAYEMS growth exceeds 150K, SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 61% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows positive acceleration; when combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS), it confirms demand-driven expansion that supports equity multiples and forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 1.5% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12901.5B in Q2 2009 shows early recovery
regime_hint: demand-led economic recovery with GDP acceleration
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 63% of instances, or the joint signal exhibits negative correlation with forward returns (Rank IC < -0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL declines or remains flat while FEDFUNDS stays near zero, creating negative real rates that compress equity discount rates and drive positive returns over 5 trading days as investors rotate into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% and CPIAUCSL at 215.79 in Sept 2009 create negative real rate environment
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with deflationary pressure
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 57% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.10 in logistic regression).
